title: Framework Benchmark Constraint
description: A single non-negotiable constraint that any candidate unified-physics framework must satisfy. Together, the entries in benchmarks/ form a falsifier set: a framework that fails any one of them is dead.

id.description: Stable slug used as the filename.

kind.description: reduction = must reproduce an established theory in a stated regime; precision_test = must reproduce a specific measurement to its quoted precision; principle_invariance = must respect a foundational symmetry to current experimental bound; forbidden_phenomenon = must not predict a phenomenon already excluded; cosmological_observable = must reproduce a cosmological observation; tension_to_address = links to existing data/ entries that the framework must explain.

requirement.description: Plain-English statement of what any candidate framework must do to pass this benchmark.

current_bound.description: Current best measurement or upper limit. Required for precision_test, principle_invariance, forbidden_phenomenon, cosmological_observable.

recovered_theory.description: For 'reduction' kind: which established theory must be reproduced (e.g. 'General Relativity', 'QED', 'Standard Model', 'LambdaCDM').

limit.description: For 'reduction' kind: the regime where the recovery is required (e.g. 'weak field, low velocity', 'energies << 1 TeV').

principle.description: For 'principle_invariance' kind: which principle is being tested.

tension_links.description: For 'tension_to_address' kind: list of data/<id> entries that any new framework must explain.

what_it_excludes.description: Plain-English statement of which frameworks or model classes are killed by this benchmark, so it is clear why this constraint matters.
